title: Crazyfile Quadrotors in Complex Envrionment
text:
Implementing a robust ROS2-based controller for agile obstacle avoidance and fast speed.


Xiyuan Zhou1, Wenjuan Lin1, Susheendhar Vijay1


1University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (UIUC)


 Paper  arXiv  Video  Code  Data

title: Crazyfile Quadrotors in Complex Envrionment
text:
Abstract

This project addresses the challenge of autonomous high-speed navigation for micro-quadrotors in constrained environments. Utilizing the Crazyflie 2.1 platform within a ROS2-based architecture, we developed an integrated control and perception pipeline designed to navigate complex, gate-based obstacle courses.
Our key contribution lies in the implementation of a robust trajectory tracking controller that bridges the gap between high-fidelity Gazebo simulations (CrazySim) and physical hardware deployment. To overcome the inherent instability of small-scale drones at high velocities, we leveraged state estimation tuning and aggressive attitude control strategies.
Experimental results demonstrate that our system achieves collision-free autonomous flight across diverse track configurations. Our optimized controller reduced lap times by [X]% (e.g., 15%) compared to the baseline PID implementation while maintaining a high safety margin. We successfully validated the sim-to-real transferability of our algorithms, transitioning from zero-collision simulated runs to consistent performance in the physical flight arena.

Related Work

Here are some related works in this area:

D4RT: Teaching AI to see the world in four dimensions introduces a unified 4D reconstruction and tracking framework. While our project focuses on ROS2-based control, D4RT represents the cutting edge of spatial perception, which is essential for high-speed autonomous navigation and “world model” understanding.
